skill.name: newsletterskill
skill.description: Guides AI agents through newsletter planning, audits, editorial calendars, sponsorship inventory, growth reviews, and retention checks.
skill.summary: Plan, audit, and improve newsletters with clear editorial structure and approval gates.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: AI review evaluated seven static findings and found them to be false positives caused by benign newsletter terminology. No prompt injection, executable code, external commands, network access, environment access, filesystem manipulation, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched ordinary newsletter wording such as description, describe, designed, and destructive. No cryptographic code, hashing API, cipher, credential handling, or executable implementation is present in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines contain prose guidance only, not code or configuration that selects a cryptographic primitive. The skill has no scripts or implementation files where weak cryptography could execute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched benign newsletter planning language. The referenced workflow asks the agent to identify a newsletter model and turn reader signals into editorial decisions, not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious locations are natural-language checklist steps about newsletter strategy. There is no command execution, host fingerprinting, shell command, or data collection instruction aimed at the local system.

content.value_statement: Newsletter teams need plans that balance editorial quality, sponsor obligations, and reader trust. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured plans, audits, and review gates for email work.
actual_capabilities: Creates issue outlines with lead items, recurring sections, sponsor slots, CTAs, and footer checks.
Builds editorial calendars that separate must-run, optional, and backlog content.
Reviews sponsor placement so paid copy stays labeled and separate from editorial copy.
Synthesizes reader signals from surveys, replies, clicks, forwards, unsubscribes, and conversions.
Defines approval gates before live sends, imports, suppression edits, DNS changes, or automation changes.
Audits cadence health using production capacity, content supply, engagement trends, churn, and commitments.
limitations: Does not send email, import contacts, edit DNS, or change production automations by itself.
Needs user-provided newsletter goals, metrics, audience context, and sponsor constraints for strong recommendations.
Does not verify subscriber consent, suppression state, or provider settings without source material.
Provides operational guidance, not legal advice about email compliance.

use_cases.title: Build an Editorial Calendar
use_cases.description: Plan upcoming issues with clear promises, recurring sections, content inventory, sponsor commitments, and review checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Audit Sponsorship Inventory
use_cases.description: Map sponsor slots, labeling rules, capacity limits, and reader trust risks before selling or placing paid content.

use_cases.title: Improve Subscriber Retention
use_cases.description: Turn engagement trends, churn signals, surveys, and replies into practical retention experiments and onboarding improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan One Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan one newsletter issue for [audience]. Include the issue promise, lead item, secondary items, recurring sections, sponsor slot, CTA, and approval checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Monthly Calendar
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a four-week editorial calendar for [newsletter]. Use our cadence, content inventory, sponsor obligations, and production constraints. Mark must-run, optional, and backlog items.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Growth and Retention
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit our newsletter growth and retention. Review opens, clicks, replies, forwards, unsubscribe reasons, survey themes, and paid conversions. Recommend experiments with review gates.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Provider Change Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review a proposed newsletter provider or automation change. Separate recommendations from execution, list risks, identify required approvals, and define QA before any live-system action.

output_examples.input: Plan next week's founder-led newsletter for product managers.
output_examples.output: An issue plan with one central promise, a lead essay, two supporting links, a labeled sponsor slot, one CTA, and send approval checks.

output_examples.input: Audit our sponsor placements for the next month.
output_examples.output: A sponsor inventory review that flags crowded issues, confirms paid labeling, separates editorial copy, and lists approval needs.

output_examples.input: Review churn signals and suggest retention work.
output_examples.output: A retention audit that groups survey themes, unsubscribe reasons, reply patterns, and engagement drops into prioritized experiments.

best_practices: Provide audience, cadence, recent issues, engagement metrics, sponsor commitments, and production constraints before asking for plans.
Separate analysis, recommendations, and live-system actions in every workflow.
Require explicit human approval before sends, imports, suppression changes, DNS edits, provider migrations, or automation changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to send campaigns or alter production systems without a human approval step.
Do not treat sponsor copy as editorial copy or hide paid placement from readers.
Do not invent missing consent, suppression, engagement, or sponsor data when source material is absent.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, audit, and improve newsletters through editorial structure, sponsor inventory, growth reviews, retention checks, and approval gates.

faq.question: Can it send newsletter campaigns?
faq.answer: No. It separates recommendations from execution and requires explicit approval before live sends or production changes.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide audience, positioning, cadence, recent issues, metrics, sponsor commitments, content inventory, and known constraints.

faq.question: Does it replace compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It can flag operational approval gates, but legal and compliance decisions still need qualified human review.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The reviewed files contain prose guidance only. Static findings were false positives, and no executable risky behavior was found.
